opportunities.rationale: BioNTech fell 17% on Mar 10 after lowering COVID vaccine revenue guidance, masking a $2.1B+ oncology pipeline with multiple Phase 2/3 readouts in H1 2026. Category B event-driven crash (guidance miss, not structural). Price stabilised in the 89-91 range for 3 sessions (Mar 19: $89.31, Mar 20: $89.27). Citi PT $130 implies 46% upside. 4/5 recovery signals: volume declining post-crash, price held above $89 for 2+ sessions, analyst consensus Buy with PT well above spot, RSI near 28 at crash low now recovering. Entry zone $87-92 with R:R 1:2.8 from midpoint $89.50. Invalidated on daily close below $84.

MongoDB dropped 27% on Mar 3 after softer FY27 guidance overshadowed a Q4 revenue beat (+27% YoY). Category B event-driven (guidance miss, not structural — Atlas cloud growth above 30% YoY intact). Price formed a stabilisation base at $258-272 over 7 sessions (Mar 13: $260.50, Mar 17: $272.27, Mar 20: $261.16). Jefferies and JPMorgan maintained Buy with PTs $310-325. 4/5 recovery signals: selling volume exhaustion post Mar 3, price holding above $258 crash low for 7+ sessions, 2 major brokers maintained/raised PT, RSI recovered from sub-25 crash level. Entry midpoint $261.50, R:R 1:2.3. Invalidated below $240.

Sea Limited crashed 24% on Mar 3 after Q4 EPS miss despite revenue beat, then continued sliding from $86 to $78.85 as EM risk-off sentiment (Iran, USD strength) compounded selling. Category B event-driven (EPS miss on heavy investment spend, revenue and EBITDA growth intact). WARNING: price NOT yet stabilised — still making lower lows ($86 to $78.85 over 5 sessions). LOW conviction: 2-session stabilisation NOT yet confirmed. Enter ONLY on confirmed daily close above $80 held for 2 sessions. R:R 1:2.3 from entry midpoint $78.50. Invalidated below $72.

Dollar Tree crashed 22% on Feb 26 on Q4 miss and multi-year store restructuring guidance. Category B event-driven (execution miss with restructuring plan, not secular decline). Earnings reported Mar 16 — binary risk passed. Price bounced to $114 post-earnings before retreating to $105.92 in the broad selloff, confirming a base above the $101 crash low. In stagflation, consumers trade down to discount retail — structural demand tailwind. 4/5 recovery signals: post-earnings bounce confirmed buy interest, price held above crash low for 3+ weeks, RSI recovered from 20, analyst consensus maintains upside. R:R 1:2.3 from entry midpoint $105.50. Invalidated below $98. Note: DLTR also appears in pre-earnings file with a passed catalyst (Mar 16) — that entry should be removed per lifecycle rules, making post-crash the primary file.
